markdown.markdown: ```{r} #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false #| fig-width: 8 #| fig-height: 8 #| dpi: 150 library(tidyverse) games <- read_csv("Games.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% filter(homeScore > 0, awayScore > 0) # Calculate counts for each score combination score_counts <- games %>% count(homeScore, awayScore) # Highlight some of the most famous outlier games outliers <- tribble( ~homeScore, ~awayScore, ~label, 184, 186, "DET 186 - 184 DEN\n(1983 - Highest Scoring)", 175, 176, "SAC 176 - 175 LAC\n(2023 - 2OT Thriller)", 18, 19, "FTW 19 - 18 MIN\n(1950 - Lowest Scoring)" ) # Plot the heatmap ggplot() + geom_tile(data = score_counts, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore, fill = n), width = 1, height = 1) + scale_fill_gradientn( colors = c("#2c1e50", "#7f1e7f", "#d83c32", "#f58a24", "#ffd13b", "#ffffff"), trans = "log10", name = "Game Count", breaks = c(1, 5, 20, 100, 500), labels = c("1", "5", "20", "100", "500+") ) + # Label font size suited for a standard width plot geom_text(data = outliers, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore, label = label), color = "black", size = 2.8, fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.7, lineheight = 0.9) + geom_point(data = outliers, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore), color = "black", size = 1.5, shape = 21, fill = "red") + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 180, 20), limits = c(15, 190)) + scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 180, 20), limits = c(15, 190)) + labs( x = "Home Points", y = "Visitor Points", title = "NBA Scorigami", subtitle = "A greater density of games lies below the 45° diagonal (where Home > Visitor), illustrating the home-court advantage.", caption = "Data Source: kaggle.com | Project: SportsVizSunday" ) + # Sensible standard font sizes theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + theme( plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5), plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray30", size = 9, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)), plot.caption = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 7.5, hjust = 1, face = "italic", margin = margin(t = 10)), panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.2), panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray95", linewidth = 0.1), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11), axis.text = element_text(size = 9), legend.position = "right", legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"), legend.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"), legend.text = element_text(size = 8) ) ```
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml: ```{r} #| echo: false #| message: false #| warning: false #| fig-width: 8 #| fig-height: 8 #| dpi: 150 library(tidyverse) # Read dataset games <- read_csv("Games.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% filter(homeScore > 0, awayScore > 0) # Calculate counts for each score combination score_counts <- games %>% count(homeScore, awayScore) # Highlight some of the most famous outlier games outliers <- tribble( ~homeScore, ~awayScore, ~label, 184, 186, "DET 186 - 184 DEN\n(1983 - Highest Scoring)", 175, 176, "SAC 176 - 175 LAC\n(2023 - 2OT Thriller)", 18, 19, "FTW 19 - 18 MIN\n(1950 - Lowest Scoring)" ) # Plot the heatmap ggplot() + geom_tile(data = score_counts, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore, fill = n), width = 1, height = 1) + scale_fill_gradientn( colors = c("#2c1e50", "#7f1e7f", "#d83c32", "#f58a24", "#ffd13b", "#ffffff"), trans = "log10", name = "Game Count", breaks = c(1, 5, 20, 100, 500), labels = c("1", "5", "20", "100", "500+") ) + # Label font size suited for a standard width plot geom_text(data = outliers, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore, label = label), color = "black", size = 2.8, fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.7, lineheight = 0.9) + geom_point(data = outliers, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore), color = "black", size = 1.5, shape = 21, fill = "red") + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 180, 20), limits = c(15, 190)) + scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 180, 20), limits = c(15, 190)) + labs( x = "Home Points", y = "Visitor Points", title = "NBA Scorigami", subtitle = "A greater density of games lies below the 45° diagonal (where Home > Visitor), illustrating the home-court advantage.", caption = "Data Source: kaggle.com | Project: SportsVizSunday" ) + # Sensible standard font sizes theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + theme( plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5), plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray30", size = 9, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)), plot.caption = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 7.5, hjust = 1, face = "italic", margin = margin(t = 10)), panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.2), panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray95", linewidth = 0.1), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11), axis.text = element_text(size = 9), legend.position = "right", legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "cm"), legend.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"), legend.text = element_text(size = 8) ) ```
